generated_description: An adversary executed a third-party discovery tool, Seatbelt.exe, via the command shell to gather information about the system and its security configurations under a user context. Operating at a high integrity level, the adversary subsequently cleared the Windows event logs, specifically indicated by Event ID 1102, as a method of defense evasion to remove forensic evidence of their activities.

similar_techniques.technique_name: Indicator Removal: Clear Windows Event Logs
similar_techniques.description: Adversaries may clear Windows Event Logs to hide the activity of an intrusion. Windows Event Logs are a record of a computer's alerts and notifications. generated_description: An adversary executed a third-party security enumeration utility, `Seatbelt.exe`, with elevated privileges to collect detailed system information. The utility was invoked with the `-group=user` flag, indicating an intent to discover information pertaining to local users or user groups on the compromised host. This activity occurred under a non-system user account but with high integrity, suggesting an attempt to gain a comprehensive understanding of the environment.
